metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command to AC4, e.g. `grep -q 'git config user.name' CONTRIBUTING.md && grep -q 'git config user.name' AGENTS.md && grep -q 'git config user.name' .agents/rules/rebar.md`, so the positive-consistency claim is mechanically verifiable in-session.
evidence: AC4 text: 'CONTRIBUTING.md, canonical agent guidance, and generated/imported guidance describe one consistent DCO contract, defined concretely as: all three name "the committer's own configured git identity (git config user.name/user.email)" as the sign-off source, and none of them names a specific third party'
Unlike AC1 ('Verify: grep -q ...'), AC4 has no 'Verify:' clause with an executable command — the condition is stated in prose only.
AC5's check covers the negative case (no hardcoded identity reintroduced) but does not verify the positive case (all three documents affirmatively name git config user.name/user.email).
findings.finding: AC4 has no concrete executable proving command; it describes the desired consistency condition in prose ('all three name... the committer's own configured git identity... and none of them names a specific third party') but provides no grep, script, or structured check an agent can run to verify it in-session.
scenarios: An executor removes the hardcoded identity from all three documents, satisfying AC2 and AC5, but replaces it with generic language that doesn't actually reference git config user.name/user.email — AC4 would be violated with no failing command to catch it.
A reviewer cannot mechanically confirm AC4 is satisfied without reading three documents and making a judgment call, making the criterion non-deterministically evaluable.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC4: 'Verify: `grep -q "git config user.name" CONTRIBUTING.md && grep -q "git config user.name" AGENTS.md && grep -q "git config user.name" .agents/rules/rebar.md`' — matching the style of AC1's Verify clause.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix AC5's grep pathspec from `'docs/*.md'` to `'docs/**/*.md'` (or `-- 'docs/'` with `--include='*.md'`) so the check recursively covers all contributor-facing docs in subdirectories of docs/.
evidence: Plan AC5 states the grep scans `docs/*.md` to prevent reintroduction of hardcoded identity in contributor-facing docs.
docs/ directory listing confirms subdirectories: docs/adr/, docs/design/, docs/migrations/, docs/calibration/, docs/archive/, docs/assets/, docs/research/ — all containing .md files.
git pathspec 'docs/*.md' matches only files directly under docs/ (e.g. docs/user-guide.md), not docs/adr/0042-auto-lander.md or docs/design/*.md.
The plan's own Remediation section uses the same non-recursive glob: `'docs/*.md'`.
findings.finding: The plan's AC5 consistency-check grep command uses the git pathspec `'docs/*.md'`, but this pathspec does NOT recursively match .md files in docs/ subdirectories (e.g. docs/adr/*.md, docs/design/*.md, docs/migrations/*.md). The docs/ directory has confirmed subdirectories containing .md files. As written, the grep step would silently pass even if a hardcoded bot identity were reintroduced into any subdirectory-level contributor-facing documentation, defeating the stated purpose of the deterministic check.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC5 / Remediation section — grep command: `git grep -nE '...' -- AGENTS.md '.agents/rules/*.md' 'CONTRIBUTING.md' 'docs/*.md'`
scenarios: A contributor reintroduces `Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart` in docs/adr/0050-dco-policy.md; the make-lint grep check exits 0 and fails to catch it.
Agent guidance is added to docs/design/agent-workflow.md with the hardcoded identity; the lint check is silent.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `'docs/*.md'` with `'docs/**/*.md'` in both the Remediation grep command and the AC5 verification command. Alternatively, use `git grep -rE '...' -- AGENTS.md '.agents/rules/*.md' CONTRIBUTING.md docs/` to cover all .md files under docs/ recursively, then add `--exclude-dir` or pathspec exclusions for docs/experiments/.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace `'docs/*.md'` in the AC5 grep command (and the Remediation section) with a recursive pathspec such as `'docs/**/*.md'` (or `-- docs/` with `--include='*.md'` if using grep) so all contributor-facing Markdown under docs/ is covered, consistent with the Cascade flag's own scope statement.
evidence: AC5 grep: `git grep -nE '...' -- AGENTS.md '.agents/rules/*.md' 'CONTRIBUTING.md' 'docs/*.md'`
Remediation section repeats the same `docs/*.md` pathspec
Cascade flag says: 'verify all imported/copied agent guidance ... and any other contributor-facing guidance under docs/'
docs/ directory confirmed to contain subdirs: docs/adr/, docs/design/, docs/migrations/, docs/calibration/, docs/research/, docs/archive/ — all skipped by a non-recursive glob
In git-grep, `docs/*.md` is a literal pathspec that matches only files directly under docs/, not files in subdirectories
findings.finding: The AC5 grep pathspec `'docs/*.md'` is non-recursive and will silently miss all contributor-facing Markdown files in docs/ subdirectories (docs/adr/, docs/design/, docs/migrations/, docs/calibration/, docs/research/, etc.). The plan's own Cascade flag says to verify 'any other contributor-facing guidance under docs/', which contradicts the shallow glob.
findings.location: AC5 (consistency check grep command) and Remediation section (grep command)
scenarios: A future commit reintroduces the hardcoded identity in docs/adr/NNNN-dco-policy.md — the lint check passes (false negative) and the regression goes undetected.
A new agent guidance file is added under docs/design/ with the bot identity — the check silently misses it.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the pathspec in both the Remediation grep command and AC5 verify command from `'docs/*.md'` to `'docs/**/*.md'`. For git-grep specifically, use `git grep -nE '...' -- AGENTS.md '.agents/rules/*.md' 'CONTRIBUTING.md' 'docs/**/*.md'` or equivalently add `':(glob)docs/**/*.md'` as the pathspec.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the non-recursive `'docs/*.md'` pathspec in the AC5 grep command with a recursive form (e.g. `:(glob)docs/**/*.md` or `'docs/**/*.md'`) so the check covers all Markdown files under `docs/` — not just the top level — while still excluding `docs/experiments/` explicitly (e.g. via `git grep --exclude-dir=experiments` or a `:(exclude)docs/experiments/**` pathspec).
evidence: Plan prescribes: `git grep -nE 'joeoakhart\+bot@navapbc\.com|Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart' -- AGENTS.md '.agents/rules/*.md' 'CONTRIBUTING.md' 'docs/*.md'`
Codebase: `docs/` contains non-experiments subdirs: adr/, design/, archive/, research/, calibration/, migrations/ — none excluded, none covered by the `docs/*.md` glob
Git pathspec `docs/*.md` without `:(glob)` or `**` does not descend into subdirectories in `git grep`
Multiple prior cached review entries in .rebar/cache/plan-review/35d2-8cd6-161c-4e7f/ independently identified this same glob defect (e.g. 060aa86f, 9cf0abe2, fa162971)
findings.finding: The AC5 grep command uses `'docs/*.md'` as a git pathspec, which in git grep is non-recursive and matches only files directly under `docs/` — it silently misses all contributor-facing docs in `docs/` subdirectories (e.g. `docs/adr/*.md`, `docs/design/*.md`, `docs/archive/*.md`, `docs/migrations/*.md`) that are not in the explicitly excluded `docs/experiments/`. If the hardcoded bot identity string reappears in any such subdirectory, the lint check passes with no signal.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC5 — the git grep command `git grep -nE 'joeoakhart\+bot@navapbc\.com|Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart' -- AGENTS.md '.agents/rules/*.md' 'CONTRIBUTING.md' 'docs/*.md'`
scenarios: A future ADR in docs/adr/ references the old bot identity string as a migration note — the lint check silently passes and the string ships undetected.
Contributor guidance migrated to docs/design/ includes a copy-paste of the old AGENTS.md snippet — the check misses it.
findings.suggested_fix:
Use: `git grep -nE 'joeoakhart\+bot@navapbc\.com|Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart' -- AGENTS.md '.agents/rules/*.md' 'CONTRIBUTING.md' ':(glob,exclude)docs/experiments/**' ':(glob)docs/**/*.md'`
Or, if using a scripts/ Python helper (consistent with the existing check_docs_index.py pattern), use Python's `pathlib.Path('docs').rglob('*.md')` with an explicit filter to skip `docs/experiments/`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the `docs/*.md` glob in both the remediation grep command and AC5 with `docs/**/*.md` (or `':(glob)docs/**/*.md'` in git-grep syntax) and add `--exclude-dir` / pathspec exclusions for `docs/experiments/` so all docs subdirectories are covered.
evidence: Plan grep: `git grep -nE 'joeoakhart\+bot@navapbc\.com|Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart' -- AGENTS.md '.agents/rules/*.md' 'CONTRIBUTING.md' 'docs/*.md'`
Plan explicitly names `docs/experiments/` as an exclusion, confirming docs subdirectories exist
AC5: 'scanning AGENTS.md, .agents/rules/*.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/*.md' — same shallow glob repeated
findings.finding: The consistency-check grep command uses the glob `docs/*.md`, which in standard shell and git-grep does NOT match files in subdirectories of `docs/`. Because the plan explicitly identifies `docs/experiments/` as an exclusion (proving subdirectories exist), other contributor-facing docs nested under `docs/` (e.g. `docs/adr/`, `docs/contributing/`) would silently escape both the sweep and the lint guard, allowing the hardcoded identity to persist undetected.
findings.location: Remediation section (grep command) and AC item 5
scenarios: A future contributor adds agent guidance under docs/adr/ or docs/guides/ with the hardcoded identity; the lint check passes and the stale reference is never caught.
The sweep pass misses an existing docs subdirectory file during the initial remediation.
findings.suggested_fix: Use `git grep -nE 'joeoakhart\+bot@navapbc\.com|Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart' -- AGENTS.md '.agents/rules/*.md' CONTRIBUTING.md ':(glob,exclude)docs/experiments/**' ':(glob)docs/**/*.md'` (git pathspec syntax), and update AC5 to match.

evidence: 4 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).

verify command `grep -q 'git config user.name' AGENTS.md && grep -q 'git config user.email' AGENTS.md`.` greps the unanchored word 'git' — it substring-matches (e.g. 'review_git'); anchor it (\b, ^$, or a quoted key like '"git":')

verify command `grep -q 'git config user.name' AGENTS.md && grep -q 'git config user.email' AGENTS.md` greps the unanchored word 'git' — it substring-matches (e.g. 'review_git'); anchor it (\b, ^$, or a quoted key like '"git":')
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile AC3's prose allowlist with its proving command: either add `CLAUDE.local.md` to the grep's `--exclude-standard` / path-exclude list, or remove it from the prose allowlist so that one single source of truth governs which paths may retain the bot identity.
evidence: Plan prose in AC3: 'Any retained Joe-specific identity is explicitly scoped to the automation that owns it (e.g. infra/, .github/workflows/, CLAUDE.local.md)'
AC3 proving command: 'any match outside those prefixes [infra/, .github/workflows/, tests/, or docs/experiments/] fails the check' — CLAUDE.local.md is absent from this allowlist
Verify-command defect taxonomy item 4 (fixture invalidity) / item 2 (allowlist mismatch)
findings.finding: AC3's prose explicitly lists CLAUDE.local.md as a legitimate location for the bot identity ('Any retained Joe-specific identity is explicitly scoped to the automation that owns it (e.g. infra/, .github/workflows/, CLAUDE.local.md)'), but AC3's proving command fails any match outside the four stated prefixes (infra/, .github/workflows/, tests/, docs/experiments/). CLAUDE.local.md does not have any of those prefixes, so the proving command would produce a false failure if CLAUDE.local.md legitimately retains the bot identity.
scenarios: An executor implements the check exactly as specified; if CLAUDE.local.md retains the bot identity, the check exits non-zero even though the plan declared that location legitimate — the criterion is simultaneously satisfied and failing.

An executor excludes CLAUDE.local.md from the grep to make the check pass, but never verifies whether the file actually contains a scoped (not general-guidance) reference, silently allowing a policy violation there.
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) drop `CLAUDE.local.md` from the prose allowlist in AC3 (requiring the file to be cleaned up as part of this ticket's scope), or (b) add a fifth allowed prefix to the proving command: `git grep -nE '...' | grep -vE '^(infra|.github/workflows|tests|docs/experiments|CLAUDE.local.md)/'` and document why that file is in-scope for automation identity.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the treatment of `CLAUDE.local.md` across AC #3 (text), AC #3 (verify), and AC #5 (grep exclusions): either add `CLAUDE.local.md` to the exclusion lists in both verify steps, or remove it from AC #3's prose example — whichever matches the actual policy intent.
evidence: AC #3 text: '(e.g. `infra/`, `.github/workflows/`, `CLAUDE.local.md`)'
AC #3 verify: 'git grep -nE ... returns matches ONLY under `infra/`, `.github/workflows/`, `tests/`, or `docs/experiments/`' — `CLAUDE.local.md` absent from exclusion list
AC #5 verify grep excludes: '`infra/`, `.github/workflows/`, `tests/`, and `docs/experiments/`' — `CLAUDE.local.md` absent here too
Remediation exclusion list: '`infra/`, `.github/workflows/`, `tests/fixtures/`, `docs/experiments/`' — `CLAUDE.local.md` also absent
findings.finding: AC #3's text names `CLAUDE.local.md` as an acceptable location for retained bot identity, but neither AC #3's own verify grep nor the AC #5 consistency-check grep excludes `CLAUDE.local.md` from its scan. An implementer following AC #3's description who places bot identity in `CLAUDE.local.md` will pass the prose requirement but fail both verify steps — a direct contradiction between the criterion's text and its verification.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC #3 (text vs. verify step) and AC #5 (grep exclusion list)
scenarios: An implementer scopes the bot identity to `CLAUDE.local.md` per AC #3's guidance, then the AC #3 verify grep flags `CLAUDE.local.md` as a violation, causing the check to fail even though the prose said it was acceptable.
A future contributor adds a `CLAUDE.local.md` entry for their local automation bot; the lint check fires even though per AC #3 that is the intended location for bot-scoped identity.
findings.suggested_fix: If `CLAUDE.local.md` is legitimately an automation-owned file (like `.github/workflows/`), add it to the exclusion list in both the AC #3 verify grep and the AC #5 Makefile/script grep, and add it to the Remediation's exclusion enumeration. If it is NOT intended as an exempted location, remove it from AC #3's prose example entirely.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile AC3 and AC5: either add `CLAUDE.local.md` to the explicit exclusion list in the grep command and document it alongside `infra/`/`.github/workflows/`/`tests/`/`docs/experiments/`, or remove it from the list of valid retention locations in AC3.
evidence: AC3: 'Verify: git grep -nE ... returns matches ONLY under infra/, .github/workflows/, tests/, or docs/experiments/; any match outside those prefixes fails the check' — CLAUDE.local.md is not under any of those prefixes
AC3 remediation text: '(e.g. infra/, .github/workflows/, CLAUDE.local.md)' — names CLAUDE.local.md as valid retention
The grep consistency check scans the repo broadly and would match CLAUDE.local.md
findings.finding: AC item 3 lists `CLAUDE.local.md` as a valid retention location for the Joe Oakhart identity ('Any retained Joe-specific identity is explicitly scoped to the automation that owns it (e.g. infra/, .github/workflows/, CLAUDE.local.md)'), but the grep consistency check does NOT exclude `CLAUDE.local.md` from its scan. If `CLAUDE.local.md` legitimately retains the bot identity, it will trigger a false-positive lint failure; if it is excluded, that exclusion must be stated explicitly. This is an internal inconsistency that will strand either the lint check or the file.
findings.location: AC item 3 vs. AC item 5 / Remediation grep command
scenarios: CLAUDE.local.md legitimately retains Joe's identity for a scoped automation workflow, but the lint check flags it and breaks `make lint`, causing confusion about whether the file must be changed or the check must be updated.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a cross-platform-safe implementation for the lint check (e.g. a Python script callable as `python scripts/check_signoff.py` without POSIX-shell or `make` dependency, or document that the check is CI-only with a named CI runner OS), so the 'fails the build' guarantee holds on Windows as well as POSIX platforms.
evidence: Plan text: 'implemented as a `make lint`-integrated grep step (e.g. a target in the `Makefile`, or a script under `scripts/` invoked from `make lint`)'
Plan text: 'git grep -nE \'joeoakhart\\+bot@navapbc\\.com|Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart\' -- AGENTS.md \'.agents/rules/*.md\' …'
Supported matrix cell: 'Platform and venue: … Windows … developer workstations' — Windows is an explicitly supported platform.
On Windows with cmd.exe/PowerShell, `make` is not a native binary; a `.sh` script requires POSIX shell (WSL/MSYS2/Git Bash) not guaranteed in a vanilla Windows developer workstation setup.
findings.finding: The plan prescribes a `make lint`-integrated grep check implemented as a Makefile target or `scripts/` shell script using POSIX single-quoted regex arguments. On Windows (a named supported platform), `make` is not natively available and POSIX shell scripts (`.sh`) are not directly executable by cmd.exe or PowerShell. This means the deterministic consistency check — the regression gate the plan depends on — silently fails to run rather than enforcing the constraint.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, final bullet: 'A deterministic consistency check exists and fails the build on a reintroduced hardcoded personal sign-off: implemented as a `make lint`-integrated grep step (script or Makefile target)…'
scenarios: A rebar contributor on a Windows developer workstation without WSL or MSYS2 runs `make lint` (or its equivalent): the shell exits with 'make: command not found' or 'cannot execute .sh: No such file or directory', so the grep check never runs and a reintroduced hardcoded identity would pass undetected — directly contradicting the AC's 'fails the build' guarantee.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the shell-script/Makefile prescription with a small Python script (e.g. `scripts/check_signoff_identity.py`) that uses `subprocess.run(['git', 'grep', ...])` — Python and git are both available across all supported platforms. Document in the AC that the check is invoked via `python scripts/check_signoff_identity.py` (and optionally wrapped in a Makefile target for POSIX convenience), so Windows contributors can run it directly without `make` or a POSIX shell.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale to the Remediation section explaining why the make lint-integrated grep approach was selected (e.g. 'A grep check in make lint is preferred over a new Python script because the pattern is a single-line match with no parsing logic, consistent with how actionlint and zizmor are integrated, and adds zero new Python dependencies to the dev environment').
evidence: Plan text: 'e.g. a target in the Makefile, or a script under scripts/' — leaves the form open without justifying the choice
Codebase: scripts/ already contains check_docs_index.py, check_readme_quickstart.py, check_verify_gate_parity.py — a rich pattern of Python doc-consistency scripts invoked from make lint
Makefile lint target already invokes ruff, zizmor, actionlint — a shell grep snippet would be a fourth heterogeneous mechanism
findings.finding: The plan does not state a positive rationale for why the make lint-integrated grep approach was chosen — it only prescribes the mechanism ('implemented as a make lint-integrated grep step') without explaining why this approach fits better than, for example, a Python consistency-check script under scripts/ (the dominant pattern for doc-drift checks already in the codebase, with 20+ such scripts visible under scripts/).
findings.location: Remediation section — 'Add a deterministic consistency check: a make lint-integrated grep step (e.g. a target in the Makefile, or a script under scripts/…)'
scenarios: An implementer adds a bare Makefile snippet that is fragile on macOS (GNU vs BSD grep flag differences) when a Python script would be portable — the plan's silence on rationale leaves this choice arbitrary.

findings.finding: AC3 ('Any retained Joe-specific identity is explicitly scoped to the automation that owns it') has no concrete proving command; the criterion relies entirely on manual inspection of file placement/context, with no deterministic check that would produce evidence on success.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC3: "Verify: `git grep -nE 'joeoakhart\+bot@navapbc\.com|Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart'` returns results ONLY in paths under `infra/`, `.github/workflows/`, `tests/`, or `docs/experiments/`; any match outside those prefixes fails the check."

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the AC3/AC5 interaction for CLAUDE.local.md: either add it to the AC5 exclusion list with a note that it is git-ignored (so `git grep` already skips it, but the exclusion makes intent explicit), or add a parenthetical in the plan stating that git-ignored files are outside `git grep`'s scan scope and require no explicit exclusion.
evidence: AC3 prose: 'Any retained Joe-specific identity is explicitly scoped to the automation that owns it (e.g. infra/, .github/workflows/, CLAUDE.local.md)'
AC5 grep exclusion list: 'excluding infra/, .github/workflows/, tests/, and docs/experiments/' — CLAUDE.local.md absent
CLAUDE.md line 15: 'Machine/operator-local settings … live in the git-ignored CLAUDE.local.md'
git grep behavior: git-ignored files are untracked and silently skipped by git grep, but this is never stated in the plan
findings.finding: AC3 names CLAUDE.local.md as an acceptable location to retain the bot identity, but AC5's grep exclusion list omits CLAUDE.local.md, leaving the decision rule for this case unspecified. The plan never states that `git grep` silently skips git-ignored files (making the omission safe in practice), nor does it co-locate this reasoning with the consistency-check definition. An agent implementing or auditing the check faces an unresolved ambiguity: is CLAUDE.local.md intentionally excluded from the grep scan, or is it a gap that could allow a silent reintroduction of the hardcoded identity?
scenarios: An agent implementing the AC5 lint check adds CLAUDE.local.md to the exclusion list (unnecessary, wasting complexity) or raises a review comment that AC3 and AC5 conflict.
A reviewer auditing the check after the fact cannot determine whether CLAUDE.local.md was intentionally unscoped or accidentally omitted, causing re-review churn.

If CLAUDE.local.md ever becomes tracked (e.g., a future maintainer removes the .gitignore entry), the check silently starts failing on legitimate bot-identity content without the plan having documented why it was excluded.
findings.suggested_fix: In AC5, add CLAUDE.local.md to the exclusion list with an inline comment: `# git-ignored; git grep skips it, but listed here for explicitness`. This co-locates the decision rule with the check definition and removes ambiguity for any agent implementing or auditing the step.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
